Oracc: CAMS/GKAB — CAMS Geography of Knowledge Corpus

TCL 06, 33 [word-problems][via cams/gkab]

Top
t.e. 1t.e. 1

ina a-mat d60 u an-tu₄ liš-⸢lim⸣

(t.e. 1) By the command of Anu and Antu, may it go well!

Obverse
o 1o 1

⸢TA 1 EN 10 ša₂⸣ a-na 2-ma BALit ⸢GAR.GAR-ma 8⸣.[32 : 1 TA 8.32 LA₂-ma]

(o 1) Scale (a ladder), which is for (?) 2, from 1 to 10. Sum so that 8 [32 : take out 1 from 8 32, so that] the remaining (amount) is 8 31 : Add 8 31 to 8 32, so that 17 03 [...].

o 22

re-ḫi 8.31 : 8.31 a-na 8.32 TAB-ma 7.⸢03⸣ [...]

o 33

tam-ḫir₂-tu₄ ša₂ TA 1 GAM 1 : 1 EN 10 GAM 10 : 1.40 ki-i EN ŠIDtu₂ 1 GAM 20 : [1/3]

(o 3) Squares from 1 by 1 : 1, to 10 by 10 : 1 40. How much is the count? Multiply [1 by 0;20 : 1/3], so that 0;20 : multiply 10 by 0;40 : two thirds, so that 6;40 : 6;40 [and 0;20 are 7]. Multiply 7 by 55, so that 6 25: the count is 6 25.

o 44

DU-ma 20 : 10 GAM 40 : 2-TA ŠU-MIN-MEŠ DU-ma 6.40 : 6.40 u₃ 20 7

o 55

7 GAM 55 DU-ma 6.25 : 6.25 ŠIDtu₂

o 66

SUKUD i-gar 10 KUŠ₃ 1 KUŠ₃ ina SAG.DU i-gar pit₂-te-ma 1 KUŠ₃ iṣ-ṣi ⸢SUKUD⸣ [...]

(o 6) The height of a brick wall is 10 cubits. It is open 1 cubit from the width of the brick wall. And the height of a log is 1 cubit [...]. By how much should I be removed from the foundation of the brick wall in order to see it? The reciprocal of 1 [is 1 ...]. Multiply 1 by 10 cubits, so that 10 : you are 10 cubits removed in order to see it.

o 77

ki-i TA SUḪUŠ i-gar lu-u₂ KAR-ma lu-mur-šu₂ IGI 1-GAL₂-BI [1 ...]

o 88

1 GAM 10 KUŠ₃ DU-ma 10 : 10 KUŠ₃ KAR-ma IGImar-šu₂ [...]

o 99

1 KUŠ₃ SUKUD iṣ-ṣi ⸢10 KUŠ₃⸣ [SUKUD i-gar] ⸢IGI 1⸣-GAL₂-BI 1 : 1 GAM [...]

(o 9) The height of a log is 1 cubit. [The height of a brick wall is] 10 cubits. The reciprocal of 1 is 1 : 1 by [...].

o 1010

⸢1⸣ 2/3 KUŠ₃ A.RA₂ 1 2/3 KUŠ₃ ki-ma-a ma-ṣi ŠE.NUMUN [2.46.40 GAM 21.36 DU-ma 1]

(o 10) 1 2/3 cubits times 1 2/3 cubits. How much is the seed-measure? [Multiply 2;46 40 by 21 36, so that 1 00 00]. Multiply 1 00 00 by 0;01 48, so that 1 48. The seed is 1 hundred 8.

o 1111

1 GAM 1.48 DU-ma 1.48 1 ME 8 ŠE-MEŠ

o 1212

US₂ SAG u BAR.NUN 40 u₃ 2 A.ŠA₃ 15 US₂ [8 SAG 17 BAR.NUN]

(o 12) Length, width, and diagonal are 40, and the area is 2 00. The length is 15, [the width 8, the diagonal 17].

o 1313

SAG.KAK 5-TA.AM₃ US₂ 6 SAG.KI ki-ma-a [ma-ṣi ŠE.NUMUN (...)]

(o 13) A triangle. Each length is 5, the width 6. How [much is the seed-measure? ...] Multiply 5 by 5, the lengths, so that 25 : [multiply] 3, half of the width, [by 3, so that 9]. Take out 9 from 25, so that the remaining is 16. How much by how much should you multiply for 16 : [multiply] 4 [by 4 so that 16]. Multiply 4, the diameter, by 3, half of the width, so that 12 : the area is 12. [Multiply] 12 by 21 [36 so that] 4 19 12 : multiply 4 19 12 by 0;01 48 so that 7 46;[33 36]. The seed is 4 hundred 1 06 and a half.

o 1414

5 GAM 5 US₂-MEŠ DU-ma 25 : 3 mi-šil SAG.KI [GAM 3 DU-ma 9v]

o 1515

9v TA 25 E₁₁-ma re-ḫi 16 mi GAM mi luDU lu 16 : ⸢4⸣ [GAM 4 DU-ma 16]

o 1616

4 DAL GAM 3 mi-šil SAG.KI DU-ma 12 : 12 A.ŠA₃ 12 GAM 21.[36 DU-ma]

o 1717

4.19v.12 : 4.19v.12 GAM 1.48 DU-ma 7.⸢46⸣.[33.36 (...)]

o 1818

4 ME 1.06 u₃ mi-šil ŠE-MEŠ

o 1919

BAR.NUN UR.A 10 KUŠ₃ US₂ UR.A <<a-di>> 10 GAM 42.30 [DU-ma 7.05 US₂]

(o 19) The diagonal of a square is 10 cubits. <What is> the length of the square <<until>>? [Multiply] 10 by 0;42 30, [so that 7;05 is the length]. Multiply 7;05 by 1;25, so that [10;25 is the diagonal].

o 2020

7.05 GAM 1.25 DU-ma [1.25 BAR.NUN]

o 2121

1 KUŠ₃ US₂ 1 KUŠ₃ SAG.KI 1 KUŠ₃ SUKUD [...]

(o 21) The length is 1 cubit, the width 1 cubit, the height 1 cubit [.... Multiply] 0;05, cubits, the horizontal, by 0;05, cubits, the horizontal, [so that 0;00 25 :] multiply [0;00 25 by 1], the vertical, so that 0;00 25 : [multiply] 0;00 25 by 6 00 00, the storage, [so that 2 30 ...].

o 2222

5 KUŠ₃ na-si-ik-ti GAM 5 KUŠ₃ na-si-ik-⸢ti⸣ [DU-ma 25 : 25 GAM 1]

o 2323

⸢za⸣-qip-ti DU-ma 25 : 25 GAM 6 I₃.⸢DUB⸣ [DU-ma 2.30 ...]

o 2424

2 KUŠ₃ US₂ 2 KUŠ₃ SAG.KI 2 KUŠ₃ SUKUD [...]

(o 24) The length is 2 cubits, the width 2 cubits, the height 2 cubits [.... Multiply] 0;10, cubits, the horizontal, by 0;10, cubits, the horizontal, [so that 0;01 40 :] multiply [0;01 40 by 2, the vertical], so that 0;03 20 : multiply 0;03 20 by 6 00 00, so that [20 00 ...].

o 2525

10 KUŠ₃ na-si-ik-tu₄ GAM 10 KUŠ₃ na-si-ik-ti [DU-ma 1.40 : 1.40 GAM 2 za-qip-ti]

o 2626

DU-ma 3.20 : 3.20 GAM 6 DU-ma [20 ...]

Reverse
r 1r 1

3 KUŠ₃ US₂ 3 KUŠ₃ SAG.KI 3 [KUŠ₃ SUKUD ...]

(r 1) The length is 3 cubits, the width 3 cubits, [the height] 3 [cubits .... Multiply] 0;15, cubits, the horizontal, by 0;15, cubits, the horizontal, [so that 0;03 45] : multiply [0;03 45 by 3], the vertical, so that 0;11 15 : [multiply 0;11 15] by 6 00 00, so that [1 12 30 ...].

r 22

15 KUŠ₃ na-si-ik-ti GAM 15 KUŠ₃ na-[si-ik-ti DU-ma 3.45 : 3.45 GAM 3]

r 33

za-qip-ti DU-ma 11.15 11.15 [GAM 6 DU-ma 1.12.30 ...]

r 44

4 KUŠ₃ US₂ 4 KUŠ₃ SAG.KI 4 KUŠ₃ ⸢SUKUD⸣ [... 20 KUŠ₃ na-si-ik-ti]

(r 4) The length is 4 cubits, the width 4 cubits, the height 4 cubits [...]. Multiply [0;20, cubits, the horizontal], by 0;20, cubits, the horizontal, so that 0;06 [40 : multiply [0;06 40 by 4, the vertical, so that 0;26 40] : multiply 0;26 40 by 6 00 00, so that 2 40 00 [...].

r 55

GAM 20 na-si-ik-ti DU-ma 6.[40 : 6.40 GAM 4 za-qip-ti DU-ma 26.40]

r 66

26.40 GAM 6 DU-ma 2.40 [...]

r 77

5 KUŠ₃ US₂ 5 KUŠ₃ SAG.KI 5 KUŠ₃ SUKUD [... 25 KUŠ₃ na-si-ik-ti]

(r 7) The length is 5 cubits, the width 5 cubits, the height 5 cubits [...]. Multiply [0;25, cubits, the horizontal], by 0;25, cubits, the horizontal, so that 0;10 25 : [multiply 0;10 25 by 5, the vertical, so that 0;52 05] : multiply 0;52 05 by 6 00 00, so that 5 12 30 [...].

r 88

GAM 25 na-si-<ik>-ti DU-ma 10.25 [: 10.25 GAM 5 za-qip-ti DU-ma 52.05]

r 99

52.05 GAM 6 DU-ma 5.12.30 [...]

r 1010

IGI u IGIbu-u₂ 2.00.00.33.20 IGI u IGIbu-u₂ [ki-ma-a ma-ṣi 2.00.00.33.20]

(r 10) A reciprocal and its reciprocal are 2;00 00 33 20. [How much are] the reciprocal and its reciprocal? Multiply [2;00 00 33 20] by 0;30, so that 1;00 00 16 40 : multiply 1;00 00 16 40 by [1;00 00 16 40, so that 1;00 00 33 20 04 37 46 40]. Decrease 1 from its middle, so that the remaining is 0;00 00 33 <20> 04 37 46 40. How much [by how much should I multiply so that 0;00 00 33 <20> 04 37 46 40]? Multiply 0;00 44 43 20 by 0;00 44 43 20 so that 0;00 00 33 <20> 04 37 4[6 40 : increase 0;00 44 43 20 to 1;00 00 16 40, so that] 1;00 45 is the reciprocal. Decrease 0;00 44 43 20 from 1;00 00 16 40, [so that 0;59 15 33 20 is its reciprocal].

r 1111

GAM 30 DU-ma 1.00.00.16.40 : 1.00.00.16.40 ⸢GAM⸣ [1.00.00.16.40 DU-ma 1.00.00.33.20.4.37.46.40]

r 1212

1 TA lib₃-bi LA₂-ma re-ḫi 33.<20>.04.37.46.40 mi [GAM mi luDU-ma lu 33.<20>.04.37.46.40]

r 1313

44.33.20 GAM 44.33.20 DU-ma 33.<20>.04.37.⸢46⸣.[40 : 44.33.20 a-na 1.00.00.16.40 TAB-ma]

r 1414

1.00.45 IGIu 44.33.20 TA 1.00.00.16.40 ⸢LA₂⸣-[ma 59v.15.33.20 IGIbu-u₂]

r 1515

IGI u IGIbu-u₂ 2.03 GAM 30 DU-ma 1.02.30 [: 1.02.30 GAM 1.01.30 DU-ma 1.03.02.15]

(r 15) A reciprocal and its reciprocal are 2;03. Multiply by 0;30, so that 1;01 30 [: multiply 1;01 30 by 1;01 30, so that 1;03 02 15]. Decrease 1 from its middle, so that the remaining is 0;03 02 15. How much by how much [should I multiply so that 0;03 02 15]? Multiply 0;13 30 by 0;13 30 «by» so that 0;03 02 15 : [increase] 0;13 30 [to 1;01 30, so that 1;15 is the reciprocal]. Decrease 0;13 30 from 1;01 30, so that 0;48 is its reciprocal.

r 1616

1 TA lib₃-bi LA₂-ma re-ḫi 3.02.15 mi GAM mi [luDU-ma lu 3.02.15]

r 1717

13.30 GAM 13.30 <<GAM>> ⸢DU-ma⸣ 3.02.15 13.30 [a-na 1.01.30 TAB-ma 1.05 IGIu]

r 1818

13.30 TA 1.01.30 LA₂-ma 48 IGI⸢bu⸣-[u₂]

r 1919

IGI u IGIbu-u₂ 2.05.26.40 GAM 30 DU-ma 1.02.43.[20 : 1.02.43.20 GAM 1.02.43.20]

(r 19) A reciprocal and its reciprocal are 2;05 26 40. Multiply by 0;30, so that 1;02 43 [20 : multiply [1;02 43 20 by 1;02 43 20], so that 1;05 34 04 37 46 40. Decrease 1 from its middle, so that [the remaining is 0;05 34 04 37 46 40. How much by how much] should I multiply [so that 0;05 34] 04 37 46 40? [Multiply] 0;18 16 40 [by 0;18 16 40, so that 0;05 34 04 37 46 40.] Increase 0;18 16 40 to 1;02 43 20, so that 1;21 is the reciprocal. [Decrease 0;18 16 40 from 1;02 43 20, so that] the remaining is 0;44 26 40, its reciprocal.

r 2020

DU-ma 1.05!(06)34.04.37.46.40 : 1 TA lib₃-bi LA₂-⸢ma⸣ [re-ḫi 5.34.04.37.26.40 mi GAM mi]

r 2121

luDU-ma [lu 5].⸢34⸣.04.37.26.40 : 18.16.40 [GAM 18.16.40 DU-ma 5.34.04.27.26.40]

r 2222

⸢18⸣.16.40 ⸢a-na 1⸣.02.43.20 TAB-ma 1.21 IGIu 18.16.[40 TA 1.02.43.20 LA₂-ma]

r 2323

re-ḫi 44.26.40 IGIbu-u₂

r 2424

IGI u IGIbu-u₂ 2.00.15 GAM 30 DU-ma 1.00.07.30 : 1.[00.07.30 GAM 1.00.07.30 DU-ma 1.00.15.00.56.15]

(r 24) A reciprocal and its reciprocal are 2;00 15. Multiply by 0;30, so that 1;00 07 30 : [multiply] 1[;00 07 30 by 1;00 07 30, so that 1;00 15 00 56 15]. Decrease 1 from its middle, so that the remaining is 0;00 15 00 56 15. How much by how much should I multiply [so that 0;00 15 00 56 15]? Multiply 0;03 53 30 by 0;03 52 30 so that 0;00 15 00 56 15 : [Increase ] 0;03 5[2 30 to 1;00 07 30, so that] 1;04 is the reciprocal. Decrease 0;03 52 30 from 1;00 07 30, so that 0;5[6 15 is its reciprocal].

r 2525

1 TA lib₃-bi LA₂-ma re-ḫi 15.<<00>>.56.15 mi GAM mi luDU-[ma 15.00.56.15]

r 2626

3.52.30 GAM 3.52.30 DU-ma 15.<<00>>.56.15 : 3.⸢52⸣.[30 a-na 1.00.07.30 TAB-ma]

r 2727

1.04 IGI 3.52.30 TA 1.00.07.30 LA₂-ma ⸢56⸣.[15 IGIbu-u₂]

(colophon)
r 2828

[ṭup-pi] ⸢m⸣d60—AD—SI lu₂UMBISAG U₄ d60 dEN.LIL₂.LA₂ A ⸢m⸣[d60]—⸢EN-šu₂-nu⸣ [A md30—TI—ER₂]

(r 28) [Tablet of] Anu-ab-uṣur, scribe of Enuma Anu Ellil, son of [Anu-belšunu, descendant of Sin-leqi-unninni.]
